adds.rationale: Constellation Energy is the largest US nuclear operator and the direct counterparty in the first major hyperscaler-nuclear PPA of the AI era; replacing NuScale (SMR) with a cash-generating utility that already has a signed 20-year Microsoft contract upgrades the nuclear bucket from speculative developer to proven operator.

news_evidence.summary: Constellation Energy signs 20-year, 835MW power purchase agreement with Microsoft to restart Three Mile Island Unit 1 (Crane Clean Energy Center), the largest PPA in Constellation's history, with an in-service date of 2028 and approximately $1.6B in capital expenditure committed.

Amazon paid $650 million for a nuclear-powered data center from Talen Energy (Susquehanna PPA), establishing a second hyperscaler-nuclear deal within weeks of the Microsoft-Constellation announcement and confirming AI data centers are turning structurally to always-on nuclear baseload.
news_evidence.source: Yahoo Finance / CEG AI data center energy article

news_evidence.summary: Google signs long-term PPA with Kairos Power for up to 500MW of SMR-generated electricity by 2035, further cementing the structural shift of hyperscalers toward nuclear as baseload power for AI workloads; Constellation's existing operational fleet makes it the most investable expression of this theme.
news_evidence.source: MIT Technology Review / introl.com nuclear-AI data center coverage

removes.rationale: NuScale Power is a pre-revenue SMR design company ($12.4M expected full-year 2024 revenue) whose flagship commercial project was cancelled in November 2023; every hyperscaler nuclear PPA signed in Q4 2024 went to existing utility operators, not to NuScale, making CEG a far better expression of the nuclear-AI thesis.

news_evidence.summary: NuScale Power full-year 2024 revenue consensus is approximately $12.4M with an EPS of -$0.73 per share; the company remains pre-commercial with no operating reactors, relying on engineering study fees rather than power sales.

NuScale cancelled its UAMPS Carbon Free Power Project in November 2023 after subscriber offtake fell below the commercial threshold, demonstrating that SMR commercialization timelines are far longer than assumed and that the nuclear-AI PPA wave is bypassing NuScale in favor of existing operators.
news_evidence.source: NuScale SEC filings / World Nuclear News

news_evidence.summary: The two largest Q4 2024 hyperscaler nuclear deals (Microsoft-Constellation 835MW PPA and Amazon-Talen Susquehanna deal) were struck with existing nuclear utilities, not SMR developers; NuScale's 'first-mover' NRC design certification has not translated into signed PPAs or capital commitments.
news_evidence.source: Energy News Beat / Microsoft-Constellation PPA coverage

rationale_overall: The watchlist stays at the 8-ticker cap with a single like-for-like swap within the nuclear bucket: NuScale Power (SMR) out, Constellation Energy (CEG) in. NuScale is the weakest holding — a pre-revenue SMR design firm ($12.4M/yr revenue) whose only commercial project was cancelled in late 2023, while the Q4 2024 hyperscaler PPA wave (Microsoft-Constellation, AWS-Talen) passed it by entirely. Constellation is the direct and best-in-class beneficiary: largest US nuclear fleet, first-ever hyperscaler-scale nuclear PPA signed (20-year, 835MW with Microsoft, September 2024), and a stock that has tripled since the AI era began. Every other current position holds its thesis cleanly — NVDA with Blackwell in full production ramp, RKLB with record 16 Electron launches and $450M in new contracts, IONQ with 95% revenue growth and a quantum computing wave validated by Google's Willow chip breakthrough in December 2024, BOTZ capturing the robotics and automation buildup, ITA for the structural defense-rearmament wave post-Trump re-election, and GOOGL/MSFT as the core AI incumbents. The net effect is a sharper nuclear-AI thesis: the watchlist now holds the utility actually selling power to AI data centers rather than the developer still waiting for its first reactor to be built.
